key_insight: Domain-specific biomedical transformer embeddings, especially BioBERT, improved detection of elevated dosing-error risk in clinical trial text.
clinical_relevance: Better automated screening of trial protocols could help flag dosing risks earlier, supporting safer trial oversight and review workflows.
method: Encoded clinical-trial text with ClinicalBERT, PubMedBERT, BioBERT, and MedCPT plus categorical metadata.
Fed embeddings into classical models and neural networks, including logistic regression, gradient boosting, SVC, and a residual neural network.
Compared models using ROC-AUC to predict dosage error risk.
evidence_signal: BioBERT achieved ROC-AUC 0.794 with logistic regression, outperforming ClinicalBERT by 3.95%, while top models reached ROC-AUC 0.821–0.853.
watch_item: The findings are based on model performance in an experimental setting, so external validation and real-world workflow testing are still needed before deployment.
paper_title: CaresAI at CT-DEB26: Detecting Dosing Errors In Clinical Trials Using Domain-Specific Transformer Embeddings and Classification Models
